---
title: '12 things every guy should master to become a real man'
date: '2019-06-21T16:19:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '128099924297324033'
blogger_filename: '/2019/06/12-things-every-guy-should-master-to.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
---

<div class="WordSection1">
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<a href="https://basicgoodness.com/2011/12-things-every-ma-should-master-to-become-a-real-man/?unapproved=917816&amp;moderation-hash=2af452f19d45761be44b1755937dc069#comment-917816">https://basicgoodness.com/2011/12-things-every-ma-should-master-to-become-a-real-man/?unapproved=917816&amp;moderation-hash=2af452f19d45761be44b1755937dc069#comment-917816</a><o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<h1>
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; 12 things every guy should master to become a real man<o:p></o:p></h1>
<div class="entry-meta">
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif;">March 3, 2011 By  <span class="entry-author"><a href="https://basicgoodness.com/author/atalwin/"><span class="entry-author-name"><span style="color: #f04848; text-decoration: none;">Atalwin Pilon</span></span></a></span>  <span class="entry-comments-link"><a href="https://basicgoodness.com/2011/12-things-every-ma-should-master-to-become-a-real-man/#comments">69 Comments</a></span>  <o:p></o:p></span></div>
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 16.5pt;">Twice today I came across some funny internet content that was about â€˜being manlyâ€™. Since I donâ€™t believe in coincidence I take this as an omen and assume the Universe is inviting   me to write about masculinity. And of course I feel the need to balance the funny stuff and approach the topic dead seriously. But before I start doing that, let me give you the links that triggered this post. First one is â€œ<a href="http://www.tothechest.com/lifestyle/what-manly-dudes-talk-about" target="_blank" title="Got to www.tothechest.com">What   manly dudes talk about</a>â€ from www.tothechest.com, second is <a href="http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7H_AysErbBE&amp;feature=fvsr" target="_blank" title="Go to Tales Of Mere Existence on YouTube">  â€œHow to be a man?â€</a> from Tales of Mere Existence, an excellent channel on YouTube, in my opinion.<o:p></o:p></span><br />
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 16.5pt;">I do feel that itâ€™s an interesting and challenging topic. The bottom line is very simple. To be a man we have to be a mature version of a person with a Y chromosome. And everything   we donâ€™t develop doesnâ€™t mature. Our physical development takes care of itself more or less. We just need to keep eating, sleeping, drinking and breathing (although it helps tremendous if we do that properly). But if we donâ€™t take care of our psychological,   intellectual, emotional and spiritual development these aspects remain underdeveloped and therefore immature. You can do the quick scan right now: if you are a man and havenâ€™t been taking care of one or more of these terrains you can be sure that the corresponding   side of you is immature.<o:p></o:p></span><br />
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 16.5pt;">Anyway, letâ€™s get started. I typed this list in my phone while waiting for my order of Thai food. I did it off the cuff, There is no particular order nor am I having pretenses about   it being complete. On second thought and more honest: I think itâ€™s pretty complete but I was just acting humble. This is a list of features I honestly feel a mature man must own, be or do:<o:p></o:p></span><br />
<h2>
1. Be fearless<o:p></o:p></h2>
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 16.5pt;">Being fearless does not mean being without fear, being fearless means admitting your fears and going beyond them. A real man is not in denial of his fears, holding up a mask of invulnerability   but is willing to face his fears and work on them. He has the courage to do things that frighten him when the situation calls for it. A real man knows that the path towards fearlessness is endless.<o:p></o:p></span><br />
<h2>
2. Be resilient<a href="https://basicgoodness.com/2011/12-things-every-ma-should-master-to-become-a-real-man/nelsonmandela2/"><span style="color: #f04848; text-decoration: none;"></span></a><span style="color: #f04848; text-decoration: none;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/image001-750076-1d07ca7ded7e.jpg"><img alt="" border="0" id="BLOGGER_PHOTO_ID_6700039678368624722" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/image001-750076-3bd912f91582.jpg" /></a></span><o:p></o:p></h2>
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 16.5pt;">A man should be flexible and reliable at the same time. If necessary he can start all over again at any given moment. Whatever happens; his house was burnt down, his crop failed, he   lost his job, his wife had a miscarriage: even if he has mourning to do he starts all over again. A real man accepts his fate but doesnâ€™t become a victim of it.<o:p></o:p></span><br />
<h2>
3. Live, speak and listen from the heart<o:p></o:p></h2>
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 16.5pt;">A real man doesnâ€™t hide his feelings and intentions. He is not afraid to be gentle nor afraid to be sad. He is in touch with his emotions and is able to express them. He has the courage   to live his dream and the space to listen compassionately.<o:p></o:p></span><br />
<h2>
4. Own your anger<o:p></o:p></h2>
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 16.5pt;">A real man owns his anger. His anger has transformed into masculine compassion. He can be angry, strong, decisive and courageous. His anger serves his presence and the presence of   others. It is not a humiliating or destructive type of anger. The latter happens when a man is a slave of his anger, thatâ€™s the anger of the coward trying to overcompensate his feelings of inferiority. This is just as sad as a man completely disconnected from   his anger. He becomes emasculated and has no power at all. Other men donâ€™t take him seriously. Women can smell immature anger and emasculatedness from a mile away and donâ€™t find it sexy. Mature anger, on the other hand, is a big turn on.<o:p></o:p></span><br />
<h2>
5. Make meaning<o:p></o:p></h2>
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 16.5pt;">Try not to become a man of success, try to become a man of value. Einstein said that. I think thatâ€™s true and important. If you fill your days with doing something that pays the bills   but is essentially meaningless you are wasting your precious life. Yes, even if it pays the bills so handsomely that you can spend 2 months per year on adventurous holidays and eat in fancy restaurants, you are still wasting your life. &nbsp;A real man has the   balls to travel outside his comfort zone to make a sincere attempt to contribute to mankind. He works for the greater good instead of for the sake of protection of his self image and clinging to the illusion of safety.<o:p></o:p></span><br />
<h2>
6.&nbsp;Own your edge<o:p></o:p></h2>
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 16.5pt;">This life is a journey and we all are somewhere on our path. A real man is honest about where he is at in his development. He doesnâ€™t pretend to be wiser or more evolved than he actually   is nor does he shrink so that others wonâ€™t feel insecure around him. He knows his strengths and his weaknesses and not ashamed of either. That there is always work to do is a given to him. He knows where he has his work to do and is willing to listen and learn   from those who have done that work.<o:p></o:p></span><br />
<h2>
7. Be vulnerable<o:p></o:p></h2>
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 16.5pt;">A coward is always trying to hide his weak spots, a real man works on them. He is willing and able to reveal himself even in the midst of pain. He is vulnerable because he  <em><span style="font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif;">wants</span></em> to be vulnerable. By opening up in every moment he is continuously practicing his courage. Itâ€™s his way of defeating his own cowardice.<o:p></o:p></span><br />
<h2>
8. Make love passionately<o:p></o:p></h2>
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 16.5pt;">A real man does not hold back. When he makes love he opens up to all his rawness and all his tenderness and he gives his woman everything he has got. He does not shy away from intimacy.   He can penetrate his woman so hard and deeply that she can feel that he is entering her soul. He aims for her heart. He makes her feel like a princess and a porn star simultaneously.<o:p></o:p></span><br />
<h2>
9. Practice a martial art<o:p></o:p></h2>
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 16.5pt;">When push comes to shove every man should be able to defend his wife, children and honor. Just like we teach our children to swim to prevent them from drowning a man should know at   least enough basic fighting skills so he can throw a punch in case of an emergency. A real man has spent enough time in a ring or dojo to ensure a deescalating presence. He does not panic, is not easily provoked and has some strength and skills.<o:p></o:p></span><br />
<h2>
10. Have a sense of humor (donâ€™t take yourself so fucking seriously)<o:p></o:p></h2>
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 16.5pt;">A real man can hold both the utter importance and the utter unimportance of life at the same time. He can see his own failures and flaws and joke about that. Since he knows his self   image is just an image he feels no need to defend that image. He can uplift a tense situation with a joke but he isnâ€™t the immature joker that abuses humor as a tool for escaping intimacy or sabotaging intensity. A real man has the capacity to light up the   room. A real man embraces life.<o:p></o:p></span><br />
<h2>
11. Develop body, mind and spirit<o:p></o:p></h2>
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 16.5pt;">A real man is devoted to the development of body, mind and spirit. Not to stay ahead of other men, not out of fear of being defeated, but because he has more to offer when he is healthy   and present. Taking care of his body makes him fit and strong, taking care of his mind makes him clever and sharp, taking care of his spirit makes him wise, warm and compassionate. A real man knows that his body and mind are mortal but that the love he spreads   during his life remains.<o:p></o:p></span><br />
<h2>
12. Be real<o:p></o:p></h2>
<span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;arial&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 16.5pt;">In the end a real man knows that he is just a guy. He does all these things because thatâ€™s what he is: a man. He likes hanging out with other men, he might drink beer during football,   he loves his woman and he works wholeheartedly. Basically, he is just trying to do his best to make the most of life and is having some fun while doing that. His sweat is honest, his heart is open and his back is straight.<o:p></o:p></span><br />
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 125%;">
<b><span style="color: #0085ad; font-family: &quot;franklin gothic book&quot; , sans-serif;">Darrell Wolfe<o:p></o:p></span></b></div>
<div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 125%;">
<b><span style="color: #1f497d; font-family: &quot;franklin gothic book&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 9.0pt; line-height: 125%;">Triage Associate | Account Protection  <o:p></o:p></span></b></div>
<div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 125%;">
<span style="color: #1f497d; font-family: &quot;franklin gothic book&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 9.0pt; line-height: 125%;">Alliance Data  </span><span style="color: #1f497d; font-family: &quot;franklin gothic book&quot; , sans-serif;"><o:p></o:p></span></div>
<div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 125%;">
<span style="color: #1f497d; font-family: &quot;franklin gothic book&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 9.0pt; line-height: 125%;">751 W Hanley, Coeur dâ€™Alene, ID 83815<o:p></o:p></span></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<a href="http://www.knowmoresellmore.com/"><span style="color: #0085ad; font-family: &quot;franklin gothic book&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 10.0pt;">knowmoresellmore.com</span></a><span style="color: #1f497d; font-family: &quot;franklin gothic book&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 10.0pt;">   | </span><a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/2973795?trk=tyah"><span style="color: #0085ad; font-family: &quot;franklin gothic book&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 10.0pt;">LinkedIn  </span></a><span style="color: #1f497d; font-family: &quot;franklin gothic book&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 10.0pt;">|  </span><a href="https://www.facebook.com/KnowMoreSellMore"><span style="color: #0085ad; font-family: &quot;franklin gothic book&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 10.0pt;">Facebook</span></a><span style="color: #0085ad; font-family: &quot;franklin gothic book&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 10.0pt;">  </span><span style="color: #1f497d; font-family: &quot;franklin gothic book&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 10.0pt;">|  </span><a href="https://twitter.com/know_sellmore"><span style="color: #0085ad; font-family: &quot;franklin gothic book&quot; , sans-serif; font-size: 10.0pt;">Twitter</span></a><span style="font-family: &quot;franklin gothic book&quot; , sans-serif;">  <o:p></o:p></span></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
</div>
<br clear="both" />
______________________________________________________________________<br />
The information contained in this e-mail message and any attachments may be privileged and confidential. If the reader of this message is not the intended recipient or an agent responsible for delivering it to the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that any review, dissemination, distribution or copying of this communication is strictly prohibited. If you have received this communication in error, please notify the sender immediately by replying to this e-mail and delete the message and any attachments from your computer.<br />
______________________________________________________________________
